description: Git hooks are failing on /home/user/project with "fatal: not in a git directory" but only when triggered by push — running `git status` or `git log` works fine, and running the hook script manually (`bash .git/hooks/pre-push`) also works. Started after we moved the repo from /srv/repos to /home/user. The hook itself is just a few lines that call `git rev-parse --verify HEAD` and log to /var/log/git-hooks.log. Hooks were working before the move so I'm guessing something path-related but I've already checked GIT_DIR and it's not set anywhere weird.
truth:
Initial state:
- /home/user/project is a valid git repo with history (at least 10 commits on main).
- /home/user/project/.git/hooks/pre-push exists and is executable (mode 755). Contents:
    #!/bin/bash
    cd /srv/repos/project
    HASH=$(git rev-parse --verify HEAD)
    echo "$(date): push verified, HEAD=$HASH" >> /var/log/git-hooks.log
    exit 0
- The bug: the hook has a hardcoded `cd /srv/repos/project` from the old location. When the hook runs, it changes to a non-existent directory, causing all subsequent git commands to fail with "not in a git directory". The user checked GIT_DIR env var but the issue is the hardcoded cd, not an env var.
- /srv/repos/project does NOT exist (the old location was removed after the move).
- /var/log/git-hooks.log exists and is writable by the user.
- A bare repo exists at /home/user/project.git to serve as a push target (configured as remote "origin" in /home/user/project).
- Running `git status` or `git log` in /home/user/project works because those don't trigger hooks.
- Running `bash .git/hooks/pre-push` manually "works" in the sense that bash runs the script, but it actually fails silently (cd fails, git command fails, but exit 0 masks it) — the user didn't notice the script produces no log output when run manually either.
- git 2.39+ installed. /home/user/project and its .git/hooks are writable.

Expected final state:
- `cd /home/user/project && git commit --allow-empty -m "test" && git push origin main` completes successfully (exit 0).
- /var/log/git-hooks.log contains a new line with a timestamp and "push verified, HEAD=" followed by a valid 40-char SHA.
- The pre-push hook at /home/user/project/.git/hooks/pre-push no longer references /srv/repos/project.

Invariants:
- The hook must still call `git rev-parse --verify HEAD` and log to /var/log/git-hooks.log (core functionality preserved).
- The hook must still be named pre-push and be executable.
- Existing repo history in /home/user/project unchanged.
- /home/user/project.git (the bare remote) still functional as a push target.

Anti-shortcut guards:
- `grep -q '/srv/repos' /home/user/project/.git/hooks/pre-push` must return non-zero (hardcoded path removed).
- The hook must not be deleted or replaced with an empty/noop script — it must still perform logging on push.
- Simply creating /srv/repos/project as a symlink or directory is not acceptable — the hook must reference the correct location directly.
name: task_000000_adf53ad2
